courseleaf.title: Human Development, Doctor of Philosophy
courseleaf.informationtext:
Graduate Study

The interdisciplinary and interdepartmental Graduate Group in Human Development offers a program of study leading to the Ph.D. degree. The program provides education and training in lifespan study of human development, with a balance of emphasis on biological, cognitive, and socio-emotional development in context. Recipients of the degree will be prepared to teach, conduct research, and actively engage in public service in the field of human development.

Applicants seeking admissions and fellowships consideration must submit all materials by the final admissions deadline of December 1. For more details, see Human Development Graduate Group.

Degree Requirements & Program Coordinator

See Human Development.
courseleaf.quickviewtext: Through the Human Development program, obtain an understand of human behavioral development and the contexts in which they take place across the lifespan. Upon completion of the core coursework, students can choose an area of specialization from one of five focuses: biological; cognitive; socio-emotional; family, culture, and society; or research methodology.
